entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop (Cajanus cajan L. Millsp) used in intercropping systems.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop (Sorghum bicolor L. Moench) used in intercropping systems.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process by which certain plants fix atmospheric nitrogen into the soil.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain harvested per unit area.

entities.name: TOTAL DRY MATTER
entities.description: The total mass of the plant after water removal.

entities.name: TOTAL N CONTENT
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen present in the plant tissue.

entities.name: FERTILIZER N RECOVERY
entities.description: The proportion of applied nitrogen fertilizer taken up by the crop.

entities.name: NITROGEN MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of controlling the timing and rate of nitrogen fertilizer application.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same piece of land.

entities.name: SOLE CROP
entities.description: The practice of growing only one crop species in a field.

entities.name: ALFISOLS
entities.description: A type of soil typically found in semi-arid tropical areas, often low in nitrogen.

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: A type of soil typically found in semi-arid tropical areas, often low in nitrogen.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: A climatic region characterized by low and irregular rainfall.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the research study was conducted.

entities.name: EAST AFRICA
entities.description: A region where sorghum and pigeonpea are grown as intercrops.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A primary plant nutrient essential for growth and development.
